detail: Bulk commodities are sold by the tonne (cost = $/t of product). Metals are sold as metal/concentrate, so '$/t ore milled' is internal; the meaningful figure is cost per unit CONTAINED METAL (C1/AISC). Roughly a third of India's 30 critical minerals are BY-PRODUCTS with no standalone mining cost.
Cash/production cost is well below market price (e.g. copper sells >$10,000/t while C1 ~$4,400-6,000/t Cu; uranium price ~$70-95/lb vs cost $15-60/lb).

Grade, method, depth, geography, currency and by-product credits can move a single commodity's unit cost 3-5x. Treat as orientation, not precision. Many critical-mineral costs are proprietary; driver logic is more reliable than the dollar figure.
C1 = direct cash cost. AISC adds sustaining capex, deferred stripping, G&A and closure accruals (usually per lb Cu / per oz Au).

India imports >90% of most critical minerals; classifies coal/lignite/uranium/thorium as fuel minerals; grades non-coking coal G1-G17 by GCV; thorium & much REE/zircon are beach-sand (monazite/ilmenite) co-products under Atomic Energy regulation.

Cost figures are INDICATIVE ORIENTATION VALUES - not official, audited or authoritative numbers, and not prices. Provided 'as is' under MIT without warranty. Do NOT use as the sole basis for investment, procurement, valuation, regulatory or policy decisions; verify against current company guidance or a commercial cost-curve provider before any formal use.

Copper, Nickel, Tin and PGE (Platinum-Group) appear in BOTH their base/precious category and the 'Critical Minerals (India 30)' category. As of v0.2.0 the critical-category rows carry their own six-stage driver breakdown (previously blank); the fuller treatment still lives in the Base/Precious Metals entry, noted inline in each.

critical_mineral_strategy holds INDICATIVE STARTER values (criticality_score, supply_risk, economic_importance on a 1-5 scale; import_dependence_pct; qualitative reserves/producers/India status). Scores are illustrative, NOT official Ministry of Mines / GSI figures. criticality_score = mean(supply_risk, economic_importance). Validate and replace against official sources before any governance or policy use.

commodity_costs_numeric is auto-parsed from the first clean numeric range in cost_text and is NOT unit-consistent across rows (units include /t ore, /t product, /lb, /oz, /t LCE, /kg). Always filter to a single cost_unit before aggregating or charting. Rows with numeric_available = FALSE had no clean parseable range.
All current rows carry vintage = '2025-26'. The schema is year-keyed so future snapshots can be appended as additional vintages; filter by vintage to compare across time.

Figures paraphrased & compiled June 2026 for orientation. Verify against current company guidance or a cost-curve provider (Wood Mackenzie, CRU, S&P Global) before publication.

Thematic groups and strategic framing after Ministry of Mines 30 critical minerals (2023) & National Critical Mineral Mission (2025); import-dependence/reserves/producer notes indicative, drawn from GSI, USGS MCS, IEA Critical Minerals Outlook. Scores are illustrative starter values, not official.
